coredata.dc:title: Relative humidity sensor based on Vernier effect with GQDs-PVA un-fully filled in hollow core fiber
coredata.dc:creator: Zhao, Yong
Tong, Rui-jie
Chen, Mao-qing
Peng, Yun
coredata.dc:description: A new relative humidity (RH) sensor based on two cascaded Fabry-Perot interferometers (FPIs) and Vernier effect is proposed and developed. The two cascade FPIs are constructed by splicing a section of photonic crystal fiber (PCF) between a section of single mode fiber and a section of hollow core fiber (HCF), where the length of PCF is 281.43μm and the length of HCF is 490.83μm. The hollow core fiber is un-fully filled with Graphene quantum dots and polyvinyl alcohol. By adjusting the filling time, FPIs with different free spectral ranges are got. We study the three sensors whose filling time are 5 min, 10 min and 15 min, respectively. Among them, the FPI with 15mins’ filling has the highest sensitivity, which is 0.456 nm/%RH with RH changing from 19.63%RH to 78.86%RH. In addition, the experimental results indicate it has good reversibility and stability. What’s more, the sensitivity is about 4.8 times higher than that of our previous work. In brief, the compact configuration, easy fabrication, high sensitivity and good RH resolution imply the proposed sensor has good potential for RH measurement application.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Relative humidity
Optical fiber sensor
Graphene quantum dots
Polyvinyl alcohol
Hollow core fiber
Vernier effect

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure table body mmlmath acknowledge affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst primabst ref vitae

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: The fabrication of the sensing structure

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The sensing structure composed by cascade FPI.
(a) Scanning electron microscopy of GQDs, (b) Scanning electron microscopy of GQDs-PVA compounds.
The interference spectrum with 5mins’ filling. (a) spectrum of 15.07%RH; (b) spectrum of 34.48%RH; (c) spectrum of 58.48%RH; (d) spectrum of 78.48%RH; (e) spectrum of 93.37%RH.
The interference spectrum with 10mins’ filling. (a) spectrum of 25.33%RH; (b) spectrum of 38.38%RH; (c) spectrum of 58.59%RH; (d) spectrum of 63.71%RH; (e) spectrum of 83.54%RH.
The interference spectrum with 15mins’ filling. (a) spectrum of 19.63%RH; (b) spectrum of 39.08%RH; (c) spectrum of 59.33%RH; (d) spectrum of 69.59%RH; (e) spectrum of 78.86%RH.

The interference spectrum at different RH: (a) the interferometer spectrum of S1; (b) the fitting curve between wavelength and RH of S1; (c) the interferometer spectrum of S2; (d) the fitting curve between wavelength and RH of S2; (e) the interferometer spectrum of S3; (f) the fitting curve between wavelength and RH of S3.
(a) Reversibility experiment results of S3; (b) Reversibility experiment results of S3.
(a) is just made of GQDs-PVA compounds, (b) is GQDs-PVA compounds placed for 31 days.
The comparison of sensing performance between proposed sensor S3 and other RH sensors.

head.ce:title: Relative humidity sensor based on Vernier effect with GQDs-PVA un-fully filled in hollow core fiber

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Information Science and Engineering, Northeastern University, Shenyang, 110819, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Information Science and Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Key Laboratory of Synthetical Automation for Process Industries, Shenyang, 110819, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Key Laboratory of Synthetical Automation for Process Industries

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: College of Information Science and Engineering, Northeastern University, Shenyang, 110819, China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Information Science and Engineering

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A new relative humidity (RH) sensor based on two cascaded Fabry-Perot interferometers (FPIs) and Vernier effect is proposed and developed. The two cascade FPIs are constructed by splicing a section of photonic crystal fiber (PCF) between a section of single mode fiber and a section of hollow core fiber (HCF), where the length of PCF is 281.43μm and the length of HCF is 490.83μm. The hollow core fiber is un-fully filled with Graphene quantum dots and polyvinyl alcohol. By adjusting the filling time, FPIs with different free spectral ranges are got. We study the three sensors whose filling time are 5 min, 10 min and 15 min, respectively. Among them, the FPI with 15mins’ filling has the highest sensitivity, which is 0.456 nm/%RH with RH changing from 19.63%RH to 78.86%RH. In addition, the experimental results indicate it has good reversibility and stability. What’s more, the sensitivity is about 4.8 times higher than that of our previous work. In brief, the compact configuration, easy fabrication, high sensitivity and good RH resolution imply the proposed sensor has good potential for RH measurement application.

ce:section.ce:para: Relative humidity (RH) as an important chemical quantity can affect the quality of bio-pharmaceutical, preservation time of agricultural and sideline products [1], the service life of precision instrument [2] and so on [3,4]. Therefore, it is imperative to realize the accurate measurement of RH. With the development of the times, electronic RH sensors emerges, and developed rapidly during the past twenty years. However, measurement accuracy and response time will be affected by varying degrees with the increase of using time [5]. What’s more, the measurement accuracy and sensitivity will also be affected by external electro-magnetic fields [6,7]. According to the above reasons, the use of the electronic RH sensors is limited on many occasions. While optical fibers have many advantages, such as anti-electromagnetic interference, high temperature resistance, applied to all kinds of bad environment [8–12] and so on, and have been used for optical fiber sensing that have high sensitivity, wide measurement range [13,14]. At present, the RH fiber sensors are some of following: Fiber Bragg Grating (FBG), long period fiber grating, Mach-Zehnder Interferometer and Fabry-Perot interferometer (FPI). Sensors based on FBG and long period fiber grating has the advantage of stable sensing performance, however, the sensitivity is low [15]. What’s more, the expensive demodulation limits its practical application. RH sensors based on Mach-Zehnder principle have many advantages and have attracted wide concern of researchers [16–18]. In 2017 year, Haiwei Fu and his groups [19] proposed a U-shaped microfiber interferometer with low temperature cross-sensitivity for RH measurement, whose sensitivity is 0.1147 nm/%RH in the measurement range of 30 to 90%RH, the sensitivity isn’t very high, and the microfiber makes it easy fracture. In 2017 year, Diego Lopez-Torres and his companions [20] proposed a novel RH sensor with SnO2 thin film coated on the surface of photonic crystal fiber (PCF). However, the measurement sensitivity isn’t linear. What’s more, RH sensors based on Mach-Zehnder principle are transmission structure, which makes it difficult in practical application. While, FPI, as a reflective structure, has many advantages such as high sensitivity, compact size and easy making, has been adopted for RH sensing [21,22]. In 2016 year, Chengliang Wang and his groups [23] proposed a humidity sensor based on Fabry-Perot with Agarose filled, where the sensitivity was 22.5 pm/%RH with the measurement range from 42.1%RH to 66.2%RH. However, the sensitivity is so low and the measurement range is a little low. In 2017 year, Lei Liang and his groups [24] proposed a high sensitivity RH probe which is constructed by coating thin polyimide film on the end surface of single-mode fiber (SMF), the sensitivity is up to 0.98625 nm/%RH with the good linearity with the measurement range changing from 35%RH to 65%RH, but the temperature crosstalk can also be up to 188.06 pm/°C. What’s more, the measurement range is a little low, which is unable to meet the demand for wide range of measurement.

In this paper, we present a RH sensor based on Vernier effect with Graphene quantum dots (GQDs) and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA) compounds un-fully filled in hollow core fiber (HCF), the sensing structure is fabricated by splicing a section of PCF between a section of incident light SMF and a section of HCF, where the length of PCF is 281.43μm and the length of HCF is 490.83 μm. The interference spectrum can be adjusted by the filling time. In this paper, we study the interference spectrums with the filling time of 5 min, 10 min and 15 min, respectively. Experiment results reveal that the sensitivity of proposed sensor with 15mins’ filling can up to 0.456 nm/%RH with the measurement range from 19.63%RH to 78.86%RH, where the linear fitting degree R2 is 0.99 and the RH resolution is 0.04587%RH. In addition, the sensor presents good reversibility and stability. The temperature response is -0.173 nm/°C with temperature changing from 16.44°C to 33.33°C, which is about 2/5 of the RH sensitivity.

Vernier effect was first applied to Vernier calipers to improve the accuracy of length measurement, which can be applied in optical fiber sensor to improve sensitivity. In optical fiber sensing, periodic cursor interference fringes can be produced by cascaded two interferometers. While, the interference fringes will change sharply with the physical parameters (cavity length, refractive index, fringe fineness) of the cascaded interferometer. In addition, the interference fringes are periodically changed, and the sensors can be realized by demodulating the envelope of the interference fringes. In this paper, we present a RH sensor based on Vernier effect with Graphene quantum dots (GQDs) and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA) compounds un-fully filled in hollow core fiber (HCF). Fig. 1 shows the schematic diagram of the proposed sensor, which concludes three parts: a section of SMF, a section of PCF and a section of HCF un-fully filled with GQDs-PVA compounds.

The SMF, PCF and HCF are fused together sequentially, and then the GQDs-PVA compounds are un-fully filled into the core of HCF. Due to the refractive index difference of the SMF, PCF and HCF at the welding surface, there are four reflect mirrors in the sensing structure, the first one is formed by the welding surface of SMF and PCF, named M1. The second is formed by the welding surface of PCF and HCF, named M2. The third is formed by GQDs-PVA filler in HCF, named M3. The fourth is formed by GQDs-PVA at the end of HCF, named M4. When the incident light arrived at M1, partial light reflects and most of light continues to spread forward. When the propagate light arrived at M2 and M3, partial light reflects and the other light continue to transfer forward. As we can see, there are four reflectors which is introduced by refractive index difference before and after reflection. The four reflectors are M1, M2, M3 and M4. M1 is introduced by the different refractive index of the core of SMF and PCF, the core interference index of SMF is 1.4681, the interference index of PCF is 1.45, the refractive index difference is 0.0181. M2 is introduced by the different refractive index of PCF and the core of HCF, the core interference index of HCF is about 1, and the refractive index difference is 0.45. M3 is introduced by the different refractive index of the core of HCF and GQDs-PVA substance, the refractive index of GQDs-PVA substance is about 1.49, the refractive index difference is 0.49. M4 is introduced by the different refractive index of GQDs-PVA substance and air, the interference index of air is about 1, the refractive index difference is 0.49. The smallest refractive index difference is M1. Based on the principle of reflection of light, the reflection from M1 can be ignored, which has been confirmed in Ref. [22]. Therefore, the three cavities are cavity 1 formed by M2 and M3, cavity 2 formed by M3 and M4, and the cavity 3 formed by M2 and M4. The length of cavity 3 is determined by cavity 1 and cavity 2, which is equal to the length of adopted HCF in this manuscript. M2 and M3 form a FP cavity named cavity 1 and the length is L1. M3 and M4 form another FP cavity named cavity 2, and the length is L2. M2 and M4 forms a cavity 3, and the length is L3=L1+L2. If the length of L1 and L3 is close, the two FPI cascades will generate Vernier effect.

The reflection spectrum of the sensing structure is determined by the resonance modes of the three FP cavities, and the Vernier effect can occur when the L1 and L2 have the appropriate length. The specific principle is analyzed as follows. The total electric fields of the reflective spectrum can be described as [25]:
(1)
E r
=
E
i n
[
R 1
+ A
e
− j 2
ϕ 1
+ B
e
− j 2 (
ϕ 1
+
ϕ 2
)
]
Where
E
i n
represents input electrical field;
ϕ 1
,
ϕ 2
represents the phase shift of cavity 1 and cavity 2, respectively, and can be described as
ϕ 1
= 2 π
n 1
L 1
/ λ
,
ϕ 2
= 2 π
n 2
L 2
/ λ
.
n 1
is the effective refractive index, and
n 2
is the effective refractive index, λ is the wavelength of incident light. A and B can be expressed as:
(2)
A = ( 1 −
k 1
) ( 1 −
R 1
)
R 2
B = ( 1 −
k 1
) ( 1 −
k 2
) ( 1 −
R 1
) ( 1 −
R 2
)
R 3
Where
k 1
and
k 2
are the transmission loss in M1 and M2, respectively; R1, R2, and R3 represent the corresponding reflection coefficient of M2, M3 and M4, respectively; electrical field of reflective spectrum can be introduced according to formula (1) and can be described as:
(3)
I r
=
E r
E
i n
=
R 1
+
A 2
+
B 2
+ 2
R 1
B cos [ 2 (
ϕ 1
+

) ]
+ 2
R 1
A cos ( 2
ϕ 1
) + 2 A B cos ( 2
ϕ 2
)

According to formula (3), it can be seen that the electrical field of reflective spectrum is influenced by
ϕ 2
, which is determined by
n 2
and L2. In this paper, the length of L3 is 490.83μm. The length of L2 can be adjusted by the filling time of GQDs-PVA compounds. In order to generate Vernier effect, L1 and L3 should have the near length but not the same length. The length of L1 is controlled by filling time. The finally interference spectrum is a superposition of the three individual FPIs, and the FSR1 and FSR2 are the free spectral ranges (FSR) of FPI1 and FPI2, respectively, where FPI1 is formed by cavity 1 and FPI2 is formed by cavity 3. FSR1 and FSR2 can be expressed as:
(4)
F S
R 1
=
λ 2
2
n 1
L 1
,
F S
R 2
=
λ 2
2
n 2
L 3
By connecting the peaks and valleys of the high frequency interference fringes, the envelopes of the spectrum can be got and can be described as [26]:
(5)
F S
R
e n v e l o p e
=
F S
R 1
⋅ F S
R 2
F S
R 1
− F S
R 2
=
λ 2
2

L 3
−
n 1
L 1

λ 2
2
490.83
n 2

L 1

It can be seen from formula (5) that
F S
R
e n v e l o p e
is affected by n1, L1, L3 and n2. In this paper, n1 is constant and specific values are 1. Therefore, the influence factors of
F S
R
e n v e l o p e
are L3 and n2, while L1 decreases with the increase of filling time, and n2 is constant during filling process. What’s more, the initial value of L3 is 490.83μm. The length of L1 is decrease with the increase of filling time, which means
F S
R
e n v e l o p e
increases with the increase of filling time.

As for a single FPI2, the certain dip
λ m
in the spectrum under observation, its optical phase difference should be an odd number of π , and the wavelength of the dip can be expressed as:
(6)
λ m
=
4
n 2
L 2
2 m + 1
m
is
an
integer

The interaction length and the refractive index will variation with the RH changes. As a result, the resonant dip wavelength will shift. Thus the RH sensitivity of the FPI2 can be expressed as:
(7)
S
H C F
=
2 m
∂
L 2
∂ R H
n 2

2 m
∂
n 2
∂ R H

∂

1
n 2
)

The GQDs-PVA compounds will expand with the increase of RH, which will lead to the decrease of L2. And the
n 2
will increase with the increase of RH.

In view of the FPI1, the end of the PCF is closed, and L2 and n2 have almost no change with the changing of RH. Therefore, the value of the envelope wavelength shift is magnified by amplification factor and can be expressed as:
(8)
M =
F S
R 1
F S
R 1
− F S

n 2

n 1
L 1

The reciprocal of M can be expressed as:
(9)
1 M
= 1 −
n 1
L 1
n 2
L 2

In this paper, n1 and L1 is constant, L2 will decrease with the increase of GQDs-PVA compounds filling time, and n2 is almost no change. Therefore,
1 M
is decrease and M is increase with the increase of GQDs-PVA compounds filling time.

Therefore, the sensitivity of the proposed sensor can be expressed as [16]:
(10)
S
e n v e l o p e
=
S
H C F
⋅ M =

∂

L 2

)

L 2
n 2
L 2
-

L 1
Therefore, the relationship between the interference spectrum and RH is established, and the proposed sensor can be adopted for RH sensing.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The fabrication of the sensing structure
ce:section.ce:para: Before making the sensing structure, the GQDs-PVA compounds was prepared. The GQDs with the concentration of 1 mg/mL was purchased from Nanjing Xianfeng nanomaterial technology co. LTD. In this paper, the mass ratio of PVA and GQDs was 18.4, 1.45 mL GQDs and 267 mg were mixed, then stirred by a magnetic stirrer for an hour at 30°C, and the stirring speed was 3000 rad/min during the stirring process. After that, the GQDs-PVA compounds was stationary for 10 h at room temperature. Fig. 2 presents the scanning electron microscope of GQDs and GQDs-PVA compounds, which reveals the sheet-like morphology of the two materials. Compared with other humidity sensitive materials, the advantages of GQDs-PVA substance can be summarized as follows: on one hand, compared with other metal humidity sensing materials such as SnO2, GQDs-PVA substance is no toxic and pollution-free. On the other hand, compared with other nonmetallic oxides or polymers, GQDs-PVA substance has more hydroxyl, carboxyl, epoxide and carbonyl, which makes the GQDs-PVA super permeable and super adsorptive to water molecules, which makes the proposed sensor has higher sensitivity than other optical fiber humidity sensors based on other materials. What’s more, the GQDs-PVA substance is cheaper and easier to get.

In order to prepare the sensing probe, the ordinary SMF (Corning), a section of PCF (provided by YOFC Co., Ltd.), and a section of HCF (inner diameter is 51 μm and outer diameter is 125μm). The making process of the sensing probe is shown in Fig. 3 .

Firstly, a section of SMF and a section of PCF are cut flat by fiber cutter at the end face as shown in Fig. 3(a), and then put into welding machine. After adjusting the discharge strength and propelling distance of welding machine, splicing the SMF and the PCF together by manual welding as shown in Fig. 3(b), and the welding result is shown is Fig. 3(c). Secondly, cut the other end of PCF and a section of HCF flat as shown in Fig. 3(d), after then, put them in welding machine which is shown in Fig. 3(e), adjusting the welding parameter and welding them together by manual welding. Finally, cutting the other end of HCF flat, and the sensing structure is finished, which is shown in Fig. 3(g). After then the filling process of GQDs-PVA compounds into HCF.
The schematic of the experimental system is shown in Fig. 4 , which includes a broadband amplified spontaneous emission (ASE) source with wavelength ranging from 1530 to 1610 nm shown in Fig. 4(a).

An optical spectrum analyzer (OSA) whose resolution is set as 0.02 nm during all experiments shown in Fig. 4(b), the sensing probe for RH measurement, six plastic bottles half filled with lithium chloride, magnesium chloride, sodium bromide, sodium chloride, potassium chloride and nitrate of potash six saturated salt solutions, respectively, to generate different RH values, a hygrometer to record the RH valued in plastic bottles and a voltage source provides voltage for hygrometer shown in Fig. 4(d).

In this paper, all the experimental operations are carried out at room temperature. The filling of GQDs-PVA compounds is based on the capillary filling, and different filling time correspond to different filling lengths which will affect the sensor performance. In this paper, we study the sensing probes with filling time of 5 min, 10 min and 15 min, and the corresponding sensing probes are recorded as S1, S2 and S3. The interference spectrums of S1, S2 and S3 at different RH values are shown in Figs. 5–7(a–e)
, respectively. In Fig. 5, the adopted RH values were supplied by lithium chloride, magnesium chloride, sodium bromide, sodium chloride and nitrate of potash, in Fig. 6, the adopted RH values were supplied by lithium chloride, magnesium chloride, sodium bromide, sodium chloride and potassium chloride, in Fig. 7, the adopted RH values were supplied by lithium chloride, magnesium chloride, sodium bromide, sodium chloride and potassium chloride. Experiments S2 and S3 adopted same saturated salt solutions, while the RH values were different in the experiment, which was mainly due to the opening of the cover of the acrylic box when we changed the plastic bottles half filled with saturated salt solution, it took about an hour for the RH stability to be achieved in the acrylic box. In the process, factors such as the unscrewing of the lid would change the RH values of the acrylic box when the humidity environment was stable. But this RH values were calibrated with a hygrometer, so there is no need to worry about the incorrect RH values.

Although the spectrum of Figs. 6 and 7 is not smooth enough, the difference between peak value and valley value of the interference spectrum is far greater than 2 dB in Figs. 5–7, which is larger than that of Ref [8], which means the proposed sensor is enough for optical fiber sensing.

The envelope of each interference spectrum can be got by connect the interference peaks or valleys together. While, the envelope of the interference spectrum isn’t very smooth, which is mainly caused by end surface roughness of GQDs-PVA filled in HCF. Although the filling process is uncontrollable, we can smooth the envelope of interference spectrum by late data processing. In this paper, we adopted the interference valleys as envelops and the each interference envelops are shown in the corresponding interference spectrums, which reveal that the FSR of S1 and S2 are 35.59 nm and 49.503 nm, respectively. However, Fig. 7 reveals that the FSR is larger than the spectral range of light source (80 nm). Formula (5) indicate that the closer the L1 and L2, the larger the
F S
R
e n v e l o p e
is. Therefore, we can conclude that the cavity length L2 of S2 is larger than L1, and the FSR of interference spectrum increases with the increasing of filling time before L1 and L2 are equal, which is consistent with inference of formula (5).

Fig. 8 (a), (c) and (e) are the corresponding part low envelopes of the interference spectrum of S1, S2 and S3 respectively, which reveal the interference spectrum shift to longer wavelength with the increase of RH. In addition, Fig. 8(b), (d) and (f) explain the linear relationship between RH and interference wavelength of S1, S2 and S3 respectively. Fig. 8(b) reveals that the sensitivity of S1 is 0.140 nm/%RH with the RH changing from 15.07%RH to 93.37%RH, Fig. 8(d) reveals that the sensitivity of S2 is 0.172 nm/%RH with the RH changing from 25.33%RH to 83.54%RH, Fig. 8(f) reveals that the sensitivity of S3 is 0.456 nm/%RH with the RH changing from 19.63%RH to 78.86%RH.

Therefore, S3 has the highest sensitivity among the three sensors and the RH resolution of S3 is 0.04587%RH. If we increase the filling time, the sensitivity maybe increase, while the corresponding FSR will increase at the same time, which will decrease the RH measurement range and then restricts its application in high RH environment. What’s more, the quality factor of S3 is 34.756, and the resolution of S3 is 0.0438%RH where the resolution of optical spectrum analyzer is 0.02 nm. Compared with other sensors, the quality factor is not high, but S3 presented in the manuscript has a high sensitivity and resolution in the same measurement range [24,27,28]. Therefore, the proposed S3 still has application value.

In addition, we have done the reversible and stable experiment for S3, and the results are shown in Fig. 9 . Fig. 9(a) is the results of reversible experiment which reveals that there is almost no change except at 78.66%RH, where the error between RH increase and RH decrease is 3.05 nm, the fluctuation is likely introduced by reading errors of OSA and hygrometer or temperature crosstalk. Fig. 9(b) is the results of stable experiment, which is executed by putting the sensor at for 21 days, stable experiment results have some slight fluctuation and the maximum fluctuation is about 0.576 nm, which is mainly caused by temperature variation and reading errors of OSA and hygrometer. Considering the persistence of sensing performance, we should study the sensor’s performance after longer standing time. However, the phenomenon of uneven distribution of mixture was found after 31 days of stationary mixture, which is shown in Fig. 10 .

In order to verify that temperature can affect the interference spectrum, we execute temperature experiment on S3 by putting S3 in a thermostat, and the results between temperature and interference wavelength is shown in Fig. 11 , which reveals that the interference spectrum shifts to short wavelength with temperature increasing from 16.44°C to 33.3°C where the slope is -0.173 nm/ °C and the resolution is 0.1156°C, while the interference spectrum drastically shifts to short wavelength with the temperature changing from 33.3°C to 38.5°C, and then tends to be stable after temperature is higher than 38.5°C.

The downhill point occurs near 33°C and another turning point occurs near 38°C in Fig. 11 is can be explained as: The turning point near 33°C is mainly due to the decrease of the number of water molecules in the mixture GQDs-PVA and the increase of cavity length L1.

After 33°C, the rate of dehydration of GQDs-PVA compounds increased. According to formula (5), this will lead to a sharp increase of FSR, resulting in a sharp shift of the interference spectrum to the short wavelength direction. And near 38°C, the sharp change in the spectrum is mainly the change in the amount of water molecules in the mixture GQDs-PVA is no longer obvious, according to formula (5), FSR will tend to stabilize, so the interference spectrum tends to stabilize.

The response characteristic indicates that it’s better for the RH probe to be used at constant temperature to insure the measurement accuracy. While, the slight disturbance of temperature is acceptable for S3, which is mainly because the resolution of temperature is about 2.52 times than that of RH, which means the envelope wavelength of S3 should be to re-demarcate again when the RH measurement is executed at other temperature occasions. Response time is one of the criteria for evaluating the performance of the sensor. We should have provide the response time of the proposed RH sensor in the manuscript. The response time of GQDs’ to RH is 12 s [2], the response time of PVA to RH is 600 ms [22]. For this, we can infer that the response time of GQDs-PVA to RH should be fast. However, it takes 17 s to collect a complete set of data using a spectrometer in this paper, so it is difficult to provide a specific time for GQDs-PVA to respond to RH. In addition, the RH environment in this paper is provided by different saturated salt solutions. It takes 20 min to reach a stable RH environment value. So, it is also one of the difficulties to provide the response time of GQDs-PVA to RH. Therefore, measuring the response time of GQDs-PVA to RH is the focus of our next work.
In order to more clearly explain the advantages of the proposed sensor, the comparison of sensing performance between proposed sensor S3 and other RH sensors is list in Table 1 .

Carefully analysis the sensors mentioned in Table 1, we can find that sensor in Ref [27] is based on FPI, although the RH sensor has wide measurement range, the sensitivity is so low that can’t meat RH measurement need. What’s more, the sensing structure is made by adhering PVA on the end face of SMF, which isn’t detrimental to the long term use of sensors, while the un-fully filled in HCF method proposed in this paper overcomes the problem. Although the sensitivity, RH measurement range and the resolution of the sensor in Ref [28] are acceptable for RH sensing, the sensor in Ref [28] is based on Sagnac sensing principle, the sensing structure is square structure and the length of the side of the sensing structure is longer than 148 mm, which isn’t appropriate used in narrow environment. The measurement range of sensor in Ref [15] is wide, while the sensitivity is low. In addition, the signal demodulation equipment of FBG is expensive. The sensitivity of the sensor proposed in Ref [29] is up to 0.915 nm/%RH with the measurement range changing from 85%RH to 97.6%RH, while the measurement is so low. Compared with Ref [29], the sensor in Ref [24] has the higher sensitivity and wider measurement range, however, the measurement range is still relative narrow in real life and production. What’s more, the higher RH sensitivity corresponds to higher temperature sensitivity, the temperature sensitivity in Ref [24] is 0.19 nm/°C.

Compared with the RH sensors mentioned in Table 1, the proposed sensor S3 has the advantages of high sensitivity, low hysteresis, wider measurement range and 0.04587%RH resolution that have been able to meet the actual measurement needs. In addition, the sensitivity of proposed sensor S3 is about 4.8 times higher than that of our previous work which has been accepted by Journal: Sensors & Actuators: A. Physical. The temperature crosstalk problem can be overcome by adding fiber gratings with the proposed sensing structure that has been mentioned in Ref [24,30], which will be our next work. Considering the above exposition, S3 has the advantage of simple making, high sensitivity, good resolution and wide measurement range, and can be adopted in RH measurement.

In this paper, we proposed a new RH sensor with two Fabry-Perot interferometers cascade and Vernier effect. The two cascaded Fabry-Perot interferometers are fabricated by welding a section of PCF between a section of SMF and a section of HCF, and the HCF is un-fully filled with GQDs-PVA compounds, where the length of PCF and HCF are 281.43μm and 490.83μm, respectively. The filling of GQDs-PVA compounds is based on capillary action, and filling length can be adjusted by the filling time. Different filling time corresponds to different interference spectrums and different sensing performance. In this letter, we study three sensors S1, S2 and S3, where the corresponding filling time is 5 min, 10 min and 15 min, respectively. RH sensitivity of S1, S2 and S3 are 0.140 nm/%RH, 0.172 nm/%RH and 0.456 nm/%RH, respectively, which shows that S3 has the highest sensitivity with RH changing from 19.63%RH to 78.86%RH, which is about 4.8 times higher than that of our previous work and the RH resolution of S3 is 0.04587%RH. As for S3, reversibility, stability and temperature response characteristics are also studied. The reversibility experiment result shows that S3 has almost no change during RH increase and decrease process except at 78.86%RH, and the stable experiments results indicates the sensor has good stability in the 21 days. While temperature experiment reveals that interference spectrum shifts to short wavelength with temperature increasing from 16.44°C to 33.3°C, and the corresponding slope is -0.173 nm/°C, where the RH sensitivity of S3 is about 2.5 times higher than the temperature sensitivity, while the interference spectrum drastically shifts to short wavelength with the temperature increasing from 33.3°C to 38.5°C, and then tends to be stable when temperature is higher than 38.5°C. Therefore, it is better for S3 to be used at room temperature, otherwise the envelope wavelength of S3 should be re-calibration. In addition, fiber gratings also can be cascaded with the proposed S3 to overcome temperature crosstalk, where the method to overcome temperature crosstalk problem has been adopted in many literatures. The whole experiment process and results indicate the sensor has the advantage of simple making, high sensitivity and RH resolution, good reversibility and stability, which indicates the proposed sensor can be adopted for RH measurement especially in narrow and small occasions due to the compact sensing structure.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported in part by the National Natural Science Foundation of China under Grant 61425003 and 61773102, the Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities under Grant N160408001, N170407005 and part by the State Key Laboratory of Synthetical Automation for Process Industries under Grant 2013ZCX09.

ce:biography.ce:simple-para: Yong Zhao received his M.A. and Ph.D. degrees, respectively, in precision instrument & automatic measurement with laser and fiber-optic techniques from the Harbin Institute of Technology, China, in 1998 and 2001. He was awarded a first prize scholarship in 2000 by the China Instrument and Control Society and the Sintered Metal Corporation (SMC) scholarship in Japan. He was a scholarship in Japan. He was a postdoctoral fellow in the Department of Electronic Engineering of Tsinghua University from 2001 to 2003, and then worked as an associate professor in the Department of Automation, Tsinghua University of China. In 2006, he was a visiting scholar of University of Illinois in Urbana and Champagne, USA. In 2008, he was awarded as the “New Century Excellent Talents in University” by the Ministry of Education of China. In 2009, he was awarded as the “Liaoning Bai-Qian-Wan Talents” by Liaoning Province. In 2011, he was awarded by the Royal Academy of Engineering as an academic research fellow of City University London. In 2014, he was awarded by the National Science Foundation for Distinguished Young Scholars of China. In 2015, he was honored as the Yangtze River Scholar Distinguished Professor by the Ministry of Education of China. Now he is working in Northeastern University as a full professor. As the academic leader and director of his research institute, his current research interests are the development of fiber-optic sensors and device, fiber Bragg grating sensors, novel sensor materials and principles, slow light and sensor technology, optical measurement technologies. He has authored and co-authored more than 260 scientific papers and conference presentations, 23 patents, and 5 books. He is a member in the Editorial Boards of the international journals of Sensor Letters, Instrumentation Science & Technology, Journal of Sensor Technology, and Advances in Optical Technologies.

Rui-jie Tong was born in Henan, China, in December 1991. She is currently working toward the PhD degree at College of Information Science and Engineering, Northeastern University, Shenyang, China. Currently her research is focused on the design and fabrication of the photonic crystal fiber sensors and their applications.

Mao-qing Chen was born in Liaoning, China, in Oct. 1992. He received his B.A. degrees in the College of Information Science and Engineering from the Shenyang University of Technology, China, in 2015. He is now a PhD student of Northeastern University. His research interests are optical fiber sensors, optical modal interference sensors, optoelectronic measurement technology and system.

Yun Peng was born in Hebei, China, in Jan. 1992. He received his B.A. degrees in the School of Mechanical Engineering from the Hebei University of Science and Technology, China, in 2015. He received his M.A. degrees in the School of Mechanical Engineering and Automation from the Northeastern University, China, in 2017. He is now a PhD student of Northeastern University. His research interests are optical microfiber sensors, optoelectronic measurement technology and system.
